element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental combat mechanics available from start
Basic movement paired with initial combat
Direct chargers, defeated with single hit
Powerful long-range attack unlocked by holding punch button
Ranged enemies require different tactical approach
Fire-breathing attack introduces boss pattern mechanics
Time extends with power-ups; creates pacing urgency

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating enemy groups with area-effect charged attacks
Successful boss defeat and 'I'm bad!' pose celebration
typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of new enemy types with different attack patterns
Multiple enemies at close range requiring crowd control
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating intensity per level with punctuated boss climaxes. Early levels introduce mechanics; mid-levels layer enemy complexity; late levels combine challenges. Time pressure maintains constant background tension.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction through City level with basic mechanics (punch, kick, jump) and simple blue ninja enemies. Karnov boss encounter provides first significant challenge.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Truck stage onwards introduces varied enemy types (acrobats, ranged attackers). Boss roster escalates with Iron Arm, Kamui (illusions), and Animal demonstrating diverse combat patterns.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Train stage (Akaikage with kusarigama) and Cave stage (Devil Pole with bōjutsu) culminate in final Dragon Ninja confrontation aboard helicopter with compounded mechanics.
notable_spikes: Karnov boss (fire-breathing introduces environmental hazard concept)
Kamui boss (illusion mechanic requires new tactical awareness)
Final Dragon Ninja boss (helicopter arena with accumulated enemy types)

notable_level_patterns.description: Level 1 (City/Washington D.C. streets): Tutorial-paced introduction to core mechanics. Establishes beat-em-up fundamentals with basic enemy types escalating to iconic Karnov boss.
Level 2 (Truck): Moving platform arena adds spatial constraint and momentum awareness while maintaining enemy diversity.
Level 3 (Sewer): Thematic hazard environment (water, confined spaces) paired with Kamui's illusion boss mechanic. Fire-people enemies match industrial sewer setting.
Level 4 (Forest): Natural environment likely offers more lateral space than confined sewer. Animal boss represents non-ninja super warrior variant.
Level 5 (Train): Freight train provides linear path with weapon-master boss (Akaikage, kusarigama). Thematic with extended-reach enemy mechanics.

Levels 6 (Cave → Headquarters → Helicopter): Multi-phase finale. Cave stage contains Devil Pole (bōjutsu master). Dragon Ninja confrontation on helicopter represents climactic arena with culminated difficulty.
